California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 267199, "Rush Creek", Stream, Mono Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":267199,"gaz_name":"Rush Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13252850.4757, 4573021.8107) converts to lon -119.052381, lat 37.954653 — the creek's mouth at Mono Lake, matching Wikipedia's {{cite gnis|267199}} infobox mouth coordinate (37°57'17"N 119°03'09"W / 37.95472°N 119.05250°W); the file's prior coordinate (-119.0700, 37.8600) was ~10.6km away (a guessed midpoint, not GNIS-sourced) and has been corrected to this mouth coordinate.

regulations.humanSummary: Grant Lake Dam downstream to Mono Lake: open to fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Rush Creek from Grant Lake Dam downstream to Mono Lake. All year.

regulations.humanSummary: Grant Lake Dam downstream to Mono Lake: only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Grant Lake Dam downstream to Mono Lake: 0 trout — catch-and-release only
Between Silver Lake and Grant Lake: open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Rush Creek (Mono.Co.) between Silver Lake and Grant Lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag; per CCR §7.50(a), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit is also 5 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: Between Silver Lake and Grant Lake: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Rush Creek (Region 6, Mono Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of April-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
